query: Google Antigravity CLI Agent Platform integration enterprise capabilities 2026

mako.raw:
{"query":"Google Antigravity CLI Agent Platform integration enterprise capabilities 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1818,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://developers.googleblog.com/an-important-update-transitioning-gemini-cli-to-antigravity-cli/","title":"An important update: Transitioning Gemini CLI to Antigravity CLI
            
            
            - Google Developers Blog","content":"While there won't be 1:1 feature parity right out of the gate, we made sure Antigravity CLI keeps the most critical features of Gemini CLI: Agent Skills, Hooks, Subagents, and Extensions (now as Antigravity plugins).
- Faster execution: Built in Go, Antigravity CLI is snappier and more responsive.
On June 18, 2026, Gemini CLI and Gemini Code Assist IDE extensions will stop serving requests for Google AI Pro and Ultra, as well as those using it free of charge using Gemini Code Assist for individuals.
For Gemini Code Assist for GitHub, this change will also apply and on June 18, 2026, there will be no new installations on Github organizations and requests in the following weeks will stop being served.
We’ll continue to support Gemini CLI and Gemini Code Assist with access to the latest Gemini models and other updates."},{"url":"https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli/discussions/27274","title":"An important update: Transitioning Gemini CLI to Antigravity CLI · google-gemini gemini-cli · Discussion #27274 · GitHub","content":"Below I’ll summarize what this means for users of Gemini CLI: 🌟 Key changes & Timeline Antigravity CLI is available today: Built in Go, the new CLI is snappier, supports asynchronous background workflows for complex tasks, and shares a unified architecture with the new Antigravity 2.0 desktop app.
These tiers are now supported via Antigravity CLI.
This change also applies to those using Gemini CLI via Gemini Code Assist IDE extensions and Gemini Code Assist on GitHub.
Open Source: You will continue to see us work on GitHub as we keep Gemini CLI updated with latest model releases, bugs and security fixes for our enterprise customers.
Previously, when using gemini-cli, I heavily relied on authenticating via my Google AI Studio API key to access the Gemma 4 model.
To stretch your quota, you can compress what the agent reads.
On May 19, 2026 at 5:45 PM -0400, Andrea Alberti ***@***.***>, wrote: I just had a PR landed right now in Gemini CLI.
### aniruddhaadak80 Jul 5, 2026"},{"url":"https://medium.com/@thatsiemguy/google-secops-mcp-and-antigravity-a6d0af686d31","title":"Medium","content":"In this post, we’ll cover how to set up the Antigravity desktop IDE and the Antigravity CLI to connect with Google Cloud’s hosted MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers — focusing specifically on Google SecOps — and explore what’s new.
Shared State: The Antigravity desktop IDE and CLI share configuration (and API quotas!).
Antigravity supports Google Cloud’s hosted MCP servers, and uses a shared configuration file for MCP Servers for both the GUI and CLI.
"https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform"
Quick exit tip: The Antigravity CLI uses Ctrl + D to log out.
Because the CLI and GUI share the same underlying configuration file, your SecOps integration should already be listed."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Google Antigravity CLI Agent Platform integration enterprise capabilities 2026","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://virtualizationreview.com/articles/2026/05/19/google-io-26-fills-out-enterprise-agent-stack-with-managed-agents-adk-2,-d-,0.aspx","title":"Google I/O '26 Fills Out Enterprise Agent Stack with Managed Agents, ADK 2.0 -- Virtualization Review","content":"### Google I/O '26 Fills Out Enterprise Agent Stack with Managed Agents, ADK 2.0. Google used I/O 2026 to fill in more of its enterprise agent-development stack, expanding the story beyond individual tools such as Antigravity CLI to a broader set of paths for building, running and governing AI agents on Google Cloud. At Cloud Next '26, Google detailed Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform as the foundation for building, scaling, governing and optimizing agents. The Cloud Next message was that Google wants Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform to be the foundation for enterprise agents. The broader I/O follow-up is that Google is now mapping those pieces into a full stack for agent development. A Google Cloud post for agent developers described the I/O announcements as \"a unified development toolkit\" featuring Antigravity 2.0 and the Managed Agents API. The second rung is the Managed Agents API, which Google said is new at I/O. The third rung is Antigravity, Google's agent-first development platform for coding and orchestration.","score":0.8571119,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://antigravity.google/blog/google-io-2026","title":"Google Antigravity @ I/O 2026","content":"In case you missed it, we announced _a lot_ during the first day of Google I/O 2026! We announced the building blocks that take Google Antigravity from a single product to a cohesive ecosystem of products, Google’s unified platform for builders to work with agents. The **Google Antigravity SDK** is available today in preview for developers to build custom agents using the Antigravity agent harness that has been co-optimized with Gemini, all with complete control over their deployment. AI Studio Build now also uses the Antigravity agent harness, and we’ve jointly introduced a new **AI Studio Build to Antigravity export flow**, which brings over not just your code, but also the context of your full agent conversation. For the first time, we are bringing the Antigravity ecosystem to Google Cloud customers, with Antigravity 2.0 and Antigravity CLI **available to Google Cloud customers through the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform**.","score":0.834852,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://apidog.com/blog/google-antigravity-2","title":"Google Antigravity 2.0: Agent-First Dev Platform Has Landed","content":"Google Antigravity 2.0 ships at I/O 2026: standalone desktop app, CLI, SDK, Managed Agents API, and Gemini Enterprise tier.","score":0.8232293,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://developers.googleblog.com/an-important-update-transitioning-gemini-cli-to-antigravity-cli","title":"An important update: Transitioning Gemini CLI to ...","content":"# An important update: Transitioning Gemini CLI to Antigravity CLI. When we shipped Gemini CLI last year, our goal was to bring the magic of Gemini directly into your terminal. Listening to your feedback made one thing clear: we can serve you best by pouring our energy into a single product built for today's multi-agent reality. To deliver the single platform you need to build the future, we're unifying our efforts into Google Antigravity, our premier agent-first development platform, which includes a powerful server-side harness and a brand-new terminal experience: Antigravity CLI. Whether you use Gemini CLI to get quick, grounded answers, scaffold and build out a new coding project, or help provision your cloud infrastructure, you can still do all of that right in Antigravity CLI. On June 18, 2026, Gemini CLI and Gemini Code Assist IDE extensions will stop serving requests for Google AI Pro and Ultra, as well as those using it free of charge using Gemini Code Assist for individuals.","score":0.71088976,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli/discussions/27274","title":"An important update: Transitioning Gemini CLI to Antigravity CLI · google-gemini/gemini-cli · Discussion #27274 · GitHub","content":"You signed in with another tab or window. google-gemini   /  **gemini-cli**  Public. ## LyalinDotCom May 19, 2026. To deliver the single platform you need to build the future, we are transitioning our terminal experience from Gemini CLI to the brand-new Antigravity CLI. Below I’ll summarize what this means for users of Gemini CLI:  **🌟 Key changes & Timeline**   * **Antigravity CLI is available today:** Built in Go, the new CLI is snappier, supports asynchronous background workflows for complex tasks, and shares a unified architecture with the new Antigravity 2.0 desktop app. This change also applies to those using Gemini CLI via Gemini Code Assist IDE extensions and Gemini Code Assist on GitHub. If you want to try Antigravity CLI, you can use it now with your Google Cloud projects. We are here to help make the move to Antigravity CLI and Antigravity 2.0 as smooth as possible, we have put together a migration guide that includes an FAQ, and we will be releasing video walkthroughs in the coming weeks.","score":0.6685563,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.86,"request_id":"f90c1b5a-a33f-4312-a407-e90d05a543d1"}
